CCP 128.5(A). COUNSEL FOR PAYWARD, INC. IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT AARON@MINNISANDSMALLETS.COM (CC'ING ALL PARTIES). Defendants Payward, Inc Dba Krakens And Kaiser Ngs Motion To Strike Irrelevant, False Or Improper Matter In The First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Mar-11-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Christopher N. LaVigne, Esq., T:646.694.9666 and Andrew Calderon, Esq., T:213.246.2656, of PIERCE BAINBRIDGE BECK PRICE & HECHT LLP, for defendants/ moving party;
Claire Cochran, Esq., of the LAW OFFICES OF CLAIRE COCHRAN, T:415.580.6019, for plaintiff/ opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS PAYWARD, INC. D/B/A KRAKEN, KAISER NG's Motion To Strike Irrelevant, False Or Improper Matter In The First Amended Complaint, as follows:

Defendants' motion to strike is denied in its entirety. Defendants do not show that the allegations in question are irrelevant. Defendant's address is relevant to jurisdiction. Defendants argue that it is "not essential" as jurisdiction is established through other means. However, a motion to strike lies to remove "any irrelevant, false, or improper matter," not a matter that is not essential. (See Code Civ. Proc. 436(a).) Further, Defendants do not show that the other allegations are irrelevant. Plaintiff asserts employment-based claims, including discrimination, failure to prevent harassment, and wrongful termination, claims to which Plaintiff's performance and relationship with his employer are directly relevant. Finally, allegations as to Defendants' financial improprieties and the relationship between the CEO and CBO are also relevant, as the wrongful termination claims rest on Plaintiff's allegation that it was because of reporting illegal activity that he was terminated.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
